title: EVAP Purge Valve and Canister Vent Actuator Test
description: Directly activates the EVAP purge valve (canister purge solenoid) and canister vent valve via UDS RoutineControl or IO Control to verify operation, diagnose P0443/P0446 codes, and distinguish electrical valve faults from EVAP system leak issues. Valve actuation can also confirm purge flow via fuel trim response.
common_symptoms: fuel trim spike during purge on startup
strong fuel vapor smell at purge valve location
physical_solution: Locate EVAP purge valve (typically on intake manifold or vacuum line from intake to canister). Disconnect purge hose from intake manifold with engine running — should feel no vacuum at idle if purge valve is correctly closed at idle. Apply 12V directly to valve connector to verify it clicks/opens. Check valve resistance (typical 20-40 ohm). Canister vent valve (at charcoal canister): apply 12V to verify closure.

uds_services: $27 SecurityAccess — required for $2F on some OEMs
technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — $31 purge valve activation accessible via ELM on Toyota (routine $C300), VW N80 purge valve test at ECM 0x01, Ford (pre-2018), GM, Honda, Nissan pre-2019. $2F duty cycle control more restricted. Gateway blocks: 2018+ Ford SGW, 2019+ VW SFD, 2018+ Stellantis SGW, 2019+ Hyundai/Kia.
preconditions: canister and purge valve physically accessible

procedure_steps.description: $19 02 FF — read all EVAP-related DTCs. Distinguish P0443 (purge valve circuit) vs P0441 (incorrect flow) vs P0446 (vent circuit) to separate electrical faults from functional/leak faults before actuation.
$10 03 ExtendedDiagnosticSession at ECM. Send $3E 00 TesterPresent periodically to maintain session during procedure.

$31 01 [PURGE_VALVE_ROUTINE] — activate purge valve test. Toyota: $31 01 C300. VW: $31 01 [N80_ROUTINE] at ECM 0x01. GM: $31 01 [EVAP_PURGE_ROUTINE] at ECM. Listen for audible click at purge valve location — confirms electrical function.

While purge valve activated by scan tool (or manually via 12V test): connect vacuum gauge at manifold side of purge valve hose. Should see manifold vacuum (12-18 inHg) when open, zero when closed. Confirms valve is opening to manifold vacuum.

$2F [PURGE_VALVE_DID] 03 [50% duty cycle byte] — command purge valve to 50% duty cycle. Monitor Mode $01 PID $06 STFT B1 — should see STFT drop/go rich as fuel vapors purge into intake. STFT change confirms actual vapor flow through canister.

$31 01 [VENT_VALVE_ROUTINE] or $2F [VENT_VALVE_DID] — activate canister vent valve (vent to atmosphere). Verify valve closes (audible click). EVAP monitor requires vent valve to close and seal canister for vacuum decay test.
Clear EVAP DTCs with $14 FF FF FF. Run EVAP monitor drive cycle (reference readiness_monitors.json). Verify P0443/P0446 do not return. Verify EVAP monitor status completes to PASS.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: VSV (Vacuum Switching Valve) = Toyota purge valve. Routine $C300 at ECM 0x700 activates VSV. ORVR system. Common: VSV failure causing P0441 on 2AR-FE engines. ELM accessible pre-2019. $31 C300 works on most 2006+ Toyota.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: Two-way valve and EVAP purge solenoid. $31 activation at ECM sub-address. Purge valve at throttle body vacuum port. P0441 from purge valve sticking open at idle causes rich condition. ELM accessible pre-2018.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: EVAP canister purge valve (EVAP purge solenoid). $31 at ECM 0x7E0. P0443 common purge valve circuit fault. VQ35DE: purge valve under intake manifold — requires removal of upper intake for access. ELM accessible on most model years.
oem_notes.Ford: Canister Purge Valve Solenoid (CPVS). P1450 — excessive canister vacuum (stuck closed purge valve) is Ford-specific DTC. Pre-2018 ELM $31 accessible. FDRS required 2018+. F-150 purge valve location: top of engine near coil pack area.
oem_notes.GM: EVAP purge solenoid. $31 accessible via ELM on most GM. Ecotec 1.5T: purge valve on valve cover — common to stick open. P0496 high EVAP purge flow at idle — purge valve stuck open. Enhanced actuation routine via $31 at ECM.
oem_notes.BMW: N80 valve at intake manifold (equivalent to purge valve). ECM 0x12. $31 activation accessible pre-ZGW. B46/B48: purge hose routing critical — ensure no kinks. EVAP duty cycle PID via DID at ECM. F/G-series ISTA required for $2F control.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: N80 = EVAP purge valve. N115 = canister vent valve. $31 at ECM 0x01 activates N80 (test routine opens valve). Pre-SFD ELM accessible. P0441/P0443 on 2.0T TSI often N80 electrical fault. N115 at canister body, normally open, closes during EVAP leak test. ODIS required 2019+.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Canister purge solenoid. $31 at PCM. Pre-2018 ELM accessible. Jeep Wrangler: EVAP system especially prone to small leaks from loose fuel cap and cracked filler neck — P0442 small leak very common on Wrangler. wiTECH required 2018+.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Canister purge valve. $31 at ECM 0x7E0 accessible pre-2019. 2019+ gateway blocks scan tool control. Purge valve above throttle body on most models. P0443 from connector corrosion at purge valve harness. Lambda 3.3T: dual purge valves — both must be tested.
